brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is quantum-field-theoretic particle production: a partonic/scattering description in which Lorentz-contracted bubble walls pass through each other and on-shell scatterings among their constituent quanta produce heavy particles. Paper B's machinery is the construction of a regular axisymmetric metric in general relativity — an exact spacetime geometry parameterized by mass and spin that interpolates to Kerr. There is no role-for-role correspondence between a collision-driven scattering/production process and a stationary metric-construction ansatz, so they share no underlying method beyond both being physics.
